tweets.text: RT @solana: New open-source @agentcashdev router lets teams make their API discoverable and payable by agents on Solana using @x402\n\n@merit…

Laso Finance’s Agentic Commerce Opportunity

Hunter, Founder of @LasoFinance, on the upside of agentic commerce in conversation with @metaproph3t:

“Around 96% of our volume still comes from humans. Users connect MetaMask or Phantom, get their cards, and start spending.”

“But a small and growing group is on the agentic side. Around 4% of our volume already comes from AI agents. Because there is no ID upload or liveness check, an agent can get its own Laso card with a single prompt.”

“The opportunity is to let these agents become independent economic units. They can run businesses, generate revenue, and ideally become profitable.”

“As part of this raise, we’re building the tooling that will allow an agent to control its own account, send and receive funds, and interact with the fiat world.” - @huntermmonk 

Laso Finance is building financial infrastructure for both privacy-first users and autonomous agents, creating a path for AI systems to participate directly in real-world commerce.

We just open-sourced the AgentCash Router.\n\nGet started here: https://t.co/DomFZkf0Ng

every payment an agent makes on x402 rails can be screened against TRM Labs intelligence before it settles. \nonce enabled, ampersend checks counterparty wallets for sanctions exposure and ransomware links, then blocks flagged transactions before funds move. every screening decision lands in an audit trail teams can pull anytime. agents transact at machine speed while compliance runs inline at the same pace.

.@virtuals_io was live on Robinhood Chain from day one

@DineroDom0 and @ChillTRD connected the dots:

- A Virtuals core contributor tweeted in June that 20+ top-quality AI teams were lined up to launch
- That tweet went out right before Robinhood chain went live
- Robinhood then QT'd Virtuals: "Live from day one on Robinhood chain. If you're building agents, the infra's ready."
- Day-one integration means the conversation happened months ago
- Infra was ready
- Launches queued 
- Robinhood's own Earth Is Flat call put heavy emphasis on agentic finance and user-created strategies

Monitor this one closely ������

the router (which we just open-sourced) allows any existing or aspiring merchant to build the proper infrastructure to sell to agents with little to no friction\n\nthis is a huge, necessary, step towards building a more user-aligned internet that everyone can access
x402 replaces server-side key generation and handoff with a payment authorization generated directly by the client.\n\nPay. Access. Done.
������ PayAI Ideathon — July 15\n\n3 things you could build this month ������������ https://t.co/CKT0FqKYzp

2️⃣ Accept-anything middleware

x402 now takes any ERC-20 via Permit2. Great for buyers. Chaos for merchants holding 50 random tokens.

Build the layer that accepts whatever the agent pays with and settles the merchant in USDC.

One config line. Zero headaches.

1️⃣ Pay-per-read for blogs

V2 shipped @x402/paywall as a modular package. Nobody's turned it into a one-click Ghost or WordPress plugin yet.

No subs. No signups. Readers — and agents — pay cents per article.

Creator micropayments, finally real.

My ideal AI email client would borrow the concept of internal comments from Front.

For each message, the AI reads message and leaves a comment with recommended response, list of 2nd order todos, etc.

eg an email that requires something from my accountant would pre-draft the request email, update the relevant accounting tracker spreadsheet, etc.
